difficulty_levels.name: multiple_choice
difficulty_levels.description: Choose the correct translation or best matching sentence.

difficulty_levels.name: word_bank
difficulty_levels.description: Build the sentence from pre-given words.

tips.content: 'Dojść' and 'dojechać' can both mean 'to get to' in English. Use 'dojechać' for arriving by transport and 'dojść' when someone gets somewhere on foot.
After the preposition 'do', use the genitive case.
'Studiować' can mean to study in a university or to major in a subject. 'Uczyć się' is the general verb for to study or to learn.
examples: Magda chce studiować w Warszawie. = Magda wants to study in Warsaw.
Adam studiuje medycynę. = Adam studies medicine.
Uczę się do egzaminu. = I'm studying for the exam.

tips.content: 'Jakiś/jakaś/jakieś' can translate as some, any, or simply a/an depending on context. In the plural, 'jakieś' is used for non-masculine-personal nouns.
The pattern 'iść/jechać po ...' means to go somewhere to get or fetch something.
With 'iść' or 'jechać', Polish usually uses 'do' before the names of places like restaurants or cinemas. Some building names take 'na' instead.
'Ulica' means street, and in the expression 'na ulicy ...', the noun 'ulica' comes before the street name.
examples: Centrum handlowe / Galeria handlowa jest na ulicy Bolech. = The shopping centre is on Bolech Street.

tips.content: An exchange office is called 'kantor'. After 'szukać' (to look for), use the genitive case.
'Rynek' means market square, usually in a city centre, often with a town hall and surrounded by old buildings and monuments.
Use the genitive case after the adverb 'blisko'.
'PKP' stands for 'Polskie Koleje Państwowe' (the Polish State Railways).
'Dzień' and 'doba' both mean day, but 'doba' refers to the whole 24-hour period, while 'dzień' can mean daytime.
After 'z' in the sense of 'from', use the genitive case.
You may hear or use 'gdzie znajduje się...?' meaning 'where is ... located?'. The 3rd-person singular or plural forms are commonly used.
examples: Gdzie znajduje się kawiarnia? = Where is the café?
Jakie sklepy znajdują się w tym centrum handlowym? = What shops are in this shopping centre?

tips.content: Both 'pracować' and 'działać' can sometimes translate as 'to work'. 'Działać' refers to machines or systems functioning. 'Pracować' usually refers to people doing work.

sentences.en: We love the countryside. The city is too big.
sentences.pl: Kochamy wieś. Miasto jest za/zbyt duże.
polish_alternatives: Kochamy wieś. Miasto jest zbyt duże.

sentences.en: Is this the best university in Warsaw?
sentences.pl: (Czy) to (jest) najlepszy uniwersytet w Warszawie?

sentences.en: How can I get to the city centre?
sentences.pl: Jak mogę dojechać/dojść do centrum miasta?

sentences.en: This is my favourite neighbourhood. It's very nice.
sentences.pl: To jest moja ulubiona okolica. Jest bardzo ładna.
polish_alternatives: To moja ulubiona okolica. Jest bardzo ładna.

sentences.en: Is this Piotrkowska Street?
sentences.pl: (Czy) to ulica Piotrkowska?

sentences.en: Do you want to go to a pub?
sentences.pl: (Czy) chcesz iść do pubu?

sentences.en: Do you know this club in the (city) centre?
sentences.pl: (Czy) znasz ten klub w centrum (miasta)?
polish_alternatives: Znasz ten klub w centrum miasta?

sentences.en: What is the tallest building in Warsaw?
sentences.pl: Jaki jest najwyższy budynek w Warszawie?

sentences.en: I'm going to the library. It's quiet there so I can study.
sentences.pl: Idę do biblioteki. Jest tam cicho, więc mogę się uczyć.

sentences.en: The zoo offers free admission at the weekend.
sentences.pl: Zoo oferuje wolny wstęp w weekend.
english_variants: The zoo offers free admission on the weekend.

sentences.en: The tickets to the museum aren't expensive.
sentences.pl: Bilety do muzeum nie są drogie.

sentences.en: Is there a gym nearby?
sentences.pl: (Czy) tu/tutaj niedaleko / w pobliżu jest siłownia.
english_variants: Is there a gym close by?

sentences.en: When are we going to the cinema? What films are playing now?
sentences.pl: Kiedy idziemy do kina? Jakie filmy teraz grają?

sentences.en: I'm going (on foot) to the café to get a take-away coffee.
sentences.pl: Idę do kawiarni po kawę na wynos.

sentences.en: I need to go (on foot) to the bank to get some money.
sentences.pl: Muszę iść do banku po pieniądze.

sentences.en: I'm going (on foot) to the post office to send a package.
sentences.pl: Idę na pocztę, żeby wysłać paczkę.

sentences.en: We are going (on foot) shopping to the shopping centre.
sentences.pl: Idziemy do centrum handlowego / galerii handlowej na zakupy.
english_variants: We are going to the shopping centre to go shopping.

sentences.en: I live on Bagno Street.
sentences.pl: Mieszkam na ulicy Bagno.

sentences.en: Excuse me. I'm looking for an exchange office and for a shop.
sentences.pl: Przepraszam. Szukam kantoru i sklepu.

sentences.en: This neighbourhood is interesting. There's a bar and a club here.
sentences.pl: Ta okolica jest ciekawa. Jest tu/tutaj bar i klub.
polish_alternatives: Ta okolica jest ciekawa. Tutaj jest bar i klub.

sentences.en: We can go to the cinema or to the theatre. What do you prefer?
sentences.pl: Możemy iść/jechać do kina albo do teatru. Co wolisz?

sentences.en: Are you going (on foot) to the supermarket? Can you buy something for tomorrow's supper?
sentences.pl: (Czy) idziesz do supermarketu? (Czy) możesz kupić coś na jutro na kolację?
polish_alternatives: Idziesz do supermarketu? Możesz kupić coś na jutro na kolację?

sentences.en: The market square in Wroclaw is beautiful.
sentences.pl: Rynek we Wrocławiu jest piękny.

sentences.en: I'm going to the restaurant on the corner. It's open.
sentences.pl: Idę do restauracji na rogu. Jest otwarta.

sentences.en: Are there any vegetarian dishes there?
sentences.pl: (Czy) są tam wegetariańskie dania?

sentences.en: We are going (on foot) to the park near the shopping centre for a picnic.
sentences.pl: Idziemy na piknik do parku blisko centrum handlowego / galerii handlowej.

sentences.en: Does this road lead to the centre? And is there a bus stop over there?
sentences.pl: (Czy) ta droga prowadzi do centrum? I czy jest tam przystanek autobusowy?
polish_alternatives: Ta droga prowadzi do centrum? I czy jest tam przystanek autobusowy?

sentences.en: We are on the way to the train station.
sentences.pl: Jesteśmy w drodze na dworzec kolejowy/PKP.

sentences.en: Is the petrol station open 24 hours a day (lit. the whole day)?
sentences.pl: (Czy) stacja benzynowa jest otwarta całą dobę?
sentences.literal_en: Is the petrol station open the whole 24-hour period?
english_variants: Is the petrol station open 24 hours a day?

sentences.en: Is this hotel far away?
sentences.pl: (Czy) ten hotel jest daleko?

sentences.en: We want to order a taxi from the hotel to the airport.
sentences.pl: Chcemy zamówić taksówkę z hotelu na lotnisko.

sentences.en: Is the swimming pool deep?
sentences.pl: (Czy) basen jest głęboki?

sentences.en: Where is the nearest shopping centre?
sentences.pl: Gdzie jest najbliższe centrum handlowe / najbliższa galeria handlowa?

sentences.en: Unfortunately, the lift isn't working.
sentences.pl: Niestety winda nie działa.

generated_practice.prompt_en: Choose the best Polish translation of: 'I need to go to the bank to get some money.'
generated_practice.correct_answer: Muszę iść do banku po pieniądze.
distractors: Muszę iść na bank po pieniądze.
Muszę jechać do bank po pieniądze.
Muszę iść do banku na pieniądze.

generated_practice.prompt_en: Translate into Polish: 'Where is the nearest shopping centre?'
